title.en: How to leave a Korean party EARLY
title.ko: 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요! (모임 중간에 일어날 때)

description.en: Need to catch the last bus? Use the natural phrase "저 먼저 들어가 볼게요" to leave gatherings smoothly and politely!
description.ko: 모임 중간에 먼저 일어나야 할 때! 분위기를 깨지 않으면서 자연스럽고 부드럽게 인사하는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, help! The party is so much fun, but if I don't leave in 5 minutes, I'll miss the last bus! How can I say I'm leaving without killing the vibe?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 너무 당황해서 미안하다고만 하지 않아도 돼요.
content.translation: Ah, in those times, you don't have to just keep saying sorry out of panic.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국 사람들은 이럴 때 아주 자연스럽게 쓰는 마법 같은 문장이 있거든요.
content.translation: Koreans have a magic sentence they use very naturally in these situations.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is it 먼저 실례하겠습니다? I learned that in my textbook.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그건 너무 딱딱해요! 친구들이나 동료들 사이에서는. '저 먼저 들어가 볼게요.' 라고 말하는 게 가장 자연스럽고 센스 있어요!
content.translation: Well, that's too stiff! Among friends or colleagues, saying "저 먼저 들어가 볼게요" is the most natural and sensible!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 음..., 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요. This literally means "I will try going in first," right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서는 집이나 숙소로 돌아가는 걸 들어가다 라는 동사로 자주 표현해요.
content.translation: Right. In Korea, we often express returning home or to your accommodation with the verb '들어가다'.

insight.title: 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요
insight.usage_tip: '저(I)' + '먼저(First)' + '들어가다(To go home)' + '볼게요(Will try)'. This is the standard polite way to leave any social gathering without sounding cold.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요.
content.translation: I'll be heading out first.

content.speaker: John
content.text: And what should I say after that?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아쉬운 마음을 담아서 '다음에 또 봐요' 라고 덧붙여 보세요. "오늘 정말 즐거웠다" 는 뜻이 전달될 거예요.
content.translation: With a touch of regret, try adding "다음에 또 봐요". It will convey the meaning that "I really had fun today."

insight.title: 다음에 또 봐요
insight.usage_tip: '다음에(Next time)' + '또(Again)' + '봐요(See)'. A warm way to leave the door open for the next meeting.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오늘 진짜 즐거웠어요. 다음에 또 봐요!
content.translation: I really had fun today. See you again next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. We are at a noisy BBQ restaurant, and everyone is having a great time.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 이 고기 진짜 맛있죠? 우리 이거 다 먹고 노래방도 가요!
content.translation: John! This meat is really delicious, right? Let's finish this and go to Karaoke!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저도 노래방 정말 가고 싶은데... 저 내일 아침 일찍 일이 있어서요.
content.translation: Wow, I really want to go to Karaoke... but I have something early tomorrow morning.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이~ 벌써요? 벌써 가면 너무 아쉬운데!
content.translation: Eh, already? It's such a shame if you leave now!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미안해요. 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요. 오늘 진짜 즐거웠어요!
content.translation: Sorry. I'll be heading out first. I really had a great time today!

insight.title: 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요
insight.description: Using '저(I)' instead of '나' makes it polite
insight.usage_tip: Adding '-요' ending is perfect for this friendly social setting.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 내일 일이 있으면 어쩔 수 없죠. 조심히 들어가요, 존!
content.translation: Ah, if you have work tomorrow, it can't be helped. Go home safely, John!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네! 다들 재밌게 놀고 다음에 또 봐요!
content.translation: Yes! Everyone have fun, and see you again next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Using "저 먼저 들어가 볼게요" felt so much more natural than the textbook phrases!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국어는 상황에 맞는 부드러운 리액션 하나로 분위기가 확 달라진답니다.
content.translation: Right. In Korean, a single smooth reaction that fits the situation changes the atmosphere completely.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "leaving early" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요!
content.translation: I'll be heading out first!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 다음에 또 봐요!
content.translation: See you again next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오늘 정말 즐거웠어요!
content.translation: I really had a great time today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조심히 들어가세요!
content.translation: Get home safely!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 먼저 일어나서 미안해요!
content.translation: Sorry for leaving first!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 모임에서 아쉽지만 먼저 일어나야 할 때 웃으면서 말해 보세요. 저 먼저 들어가 볼게요!
content.translation: Everyone, when you have to leave a gathering early today, try saying it with a smile. "I'll be heading out first!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 밤이 늦었으니까 이제 집으로 들어가야 해요. (It's late, so I have to go home now.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be a shame / To be sorry
vocabulary.example: 친구와 일찍 헤어지는 게 너무 아쉬워요. (It's such a shame to part with my friend so early.)
막차를 놓치지 않으려면 지금 빨리 가야 해요. (I have to go quickly now so I don't miss the last bus.)
존 씨는 분위기를 좋게 만드는 리액션을 잘해요. (John is good at making reactions that improve the atmosphere.)
길이 미끄러우니까 조심히 들어가세요. (The road is slippery, so go home safely.)
오늘 존 덕분에 정말 즐거운 시간을 보냈어요. (I had a very pleasant time today thanks to John.)
